evals.prompt: Use competitor-language-x-ray on three sleep supplement brands. I want competitor funnels, offers, bad reviews, PDP/CRO notes, and the customer language from Reddit/YouTube/Amazon around people who cannot stay asleep.
evals.expected_output: A full ecommerce market x-ray with per-competitor positioning, buyer vs audience, funnel, offer stack, pricing, proof, PDP/CRO notes, links, customer-language bank, bad-review edges, avatar/sub-avatar map, angle recommendations, offer/PDP tests, and a threat ranking with evidence tiers.
assertions: Separates audience from buyer for each competitor
Includes ecommerce offer mechanics such as bundles, discounts, guarantees, subscriptions, free gifts, shipping thresholds, or warranties when found
Includes PDP/CRO and creative/ad observations when available
Mines customer language from at least three ecommerce-relevant source types when available
Clusters raw language into desires, pains, objections, failed attempts, proof demands, and review wounds
Uses CONFIRMED, REPORTED, INFERRED, and RAW VOICE appropriately
Includes sources and names gaps instead of fabricating

evals.prompt: My product category is dog water fountains for anxious pet owners. Find the exact buyer language, tone, objections, review wounds, and angle ideas. No competitors required unless needed for review mining.
evals.expected_output: A buyer-language-only ecommerce x-ray that searches category discussion surfaces, Amazon/marketplace reviews, public comments, and competitor review wounds; builds a core buyer avatar and sub-avatars; extracts buyer words/tone/objections/current workarounds/proof demands; and turns them into angle, hook, PDP, and offer directions.
assertions: Does not require competitor inputs
Searches places where ecommerce buyers actually talk, such as Reddit, forums, YouTube comments, TikTok comments, Amazon reviews, marketplace reviews, or public communities
Unpacks beliefs into experience, emotion, behavior, and desire
Creates actionable sub-avatar angle directions
Maps findings to ecommerce actions such as hooks, PDP proof blocks, FAQ/objection blocks, offer tests, or bundle tests
Distinguishes repeated patterns from isolated comments

evals.prompt: Research these Shopify stores in the red light therapy category: brand1.com, brand2.com, brand3.com. Compare their Meta ads, PDPs, Shopify apps/themes, offers, pricing, guarantees, subscriptions, review complaints, and who is the biggest threat.
evals.expected_output: An ecommerce competitor x-ray that compares store positioning, funnel paths, paid-social maturity, Shopify app/theme stack, PDP/CRO strategy, offer stacks, pricing, guarantees, subscriptions, proof, review complaints, quant signals, and threat ranking with sourced evidence tiers.
assertions: Treats the targets as ecommerce stores rather than generic businesses
Captures offer stack details such as price, bundles, discounts, shipping threshold, guarantee, subscription, and upsells when available
Reviews paid-social and creative signals when public sources are available
Includes PDP/CRO observations such as above-the-fold promise, proof blocks, objections, reviews, trust assets, and FAQ
Detects Shopify theme and visible apps/integrations when relevant, with detector source links and confidence/gaps
Separates product complaints from shipping, return, subscription, or customer-support complaints
Ranks competitors using offer strength, proof strength, paid-social maturity, momentum, and customer-language fit
